rulings.Case Title: GEORGE KELLY VS. GEORGE POON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 1. PLAINTIFF GEORGE KELLY's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint GRANTED. California's liberal policy to decide cases on the merits militates toward granting leave to amend absent a showing of prejudice. Defendants failed to show prejudice caused by granting leave to amend. Defendant contends that neither the original complaint nor the proposed first amended complaint supports a prayer for punitive damages. This contention is premature and is not contemplated on a motion for leave to amend a complaint. Defendant's motion to strike is off calendar in light of the amended complaint without prejudice to renewing it if appropriate. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: KIET KHAI MAC VS. IMPAC FUNDING CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANT IMPAC SECURED ASSETS CORP. MORTAGE PASS-THROUGH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED in its entirety. As to the second cause of action, it is not duplicative of Plaintiff's first cause of action. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff's cause of action is not duplicative and Plaintiff has properly pled damages. As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff has properly pled intent to defraud and justifiable reliance. As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff has pled specific actions that amount to outrageous conduct. As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiff has adequately pled duty. As to the seventh cause of action, Defendant attempts to contradict facts properly pled in the complaint, factual arguments are not proper in a demurrer. As to the eighth cause of action, as the other causes of action have not failed, this cause does not fail. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. BERNETTA ADOLPH et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 7. DEFENDANT BERNETTA ADOLPH's Motion To Vacate Judgment OFF CALENDAR for insufficient notice. Even if it had been hand served on the date it was filed, the earliest hearing date would have been May 22, 2013, but it was noticed for May 15, 2013. If it had been served by mail, the earliest date would have been May 27, 2013. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: 1320-1380 LOMBARD STREET, LLC VS. LETICIA GONZALES et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 8. DEFENDANT LETICIA GONZALES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Plaintiff has not shown that an action by the Director of the Department of Building Inspection is not a prerequisite for the lessor bringing a civil action. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: 1320-1380 LOMBARD STREET, LLC VS. LETICIA GONZALES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 8. PLAINTIFF 1320-1380 LOMBARD STREET, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Defendant has raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant violated the Residential Unit Conversion Ordinance. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. CORA JOHANSEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 3. DEFENDANT CORA JOHANSEN'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT GRANTED. Defendant has shown that default was entered through her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect, and she moved diligently to address the problem. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: EPMI, A BAYSIDE COMPANY VS. MELISSA GONZALEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT MELISSA GONZALEZ'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT GRANTED in light of Defendant's diligence once she understood the circumstances surrounding her eviction. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: TUAN LE VS. DAVID LAWSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 5. PLAINTIFF TUAN LE's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft The matter is off calendar per moving party. = (501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: RECOVERY SURVIVAL NETWORK VS. RAYMOND BENNETT et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 21, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANT RAYMOND BENNETT MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT GRANTED. Verification does not establish that the complaint was verified by an officer of the corporation. = (501/PJB)
